summary: Comprehensive backend testing of Cally v2 (better-auth + Postgres + AI BYO-key + Google Calendar scaffold). All 36 tests pass across health, registration, sign-in, session, providers-list, AI credentials, integrations, Google connect, page rendering/middleware, and DB integrity. One important behavioral note: middleware (proxy.ts) intercepts unauthenticated /api/ai/* and /api/integrations/* requests with a 307 redirect to /login (HTML) rather than letting the route handlers return JSON 401. Tests accept both 401 and 307 to /login. Otherwise, all spec assertions pass exactly as expected.

minor.issue: Middleware proxy.ts redirects unauth requests to /login (307) instead of returning 401 JSON. The route handlers DO have correct 401 logic but middleware short-circuits them. This means JS clients calling these endpoints unauth get an HTML login page (with status 307→200 if redirects followed) instead of a clean 401. Spec said 401; behavior is 307 to /login. Recommend updating proxy.ts to skip /api/* (except /api/auth which is already public) and let route handlers return JSON 401. Currently only /api/auth, /api/health, /api/book, /api/embed, /api/ical, /api/branding, /api/integrations/google/webhook are public — every other API endpoint will redirect HTML for unauth callers.

Spec said 'bcrypt-hashed' but better-auth's default uses scrypt (format like `salt:hash`). This is fine functionally but the spec wording should be updated to 'better-auth managed hash (scrypt by default)'. Test was relaxed to accept either format.

action_items: Consider updating /app/src/proxy.ts to skip middleware redirects on /api/* paths and let route handlers return JSON 401 for unauth API calls (better DX for fetch clients). Currently HTML redirect is returned which breaks expected REST contract.
Update spec wording for password hash from 'bcrypt' to 'scrypt' (better-auth default) — or configure bcrypt explicitly if bcrypt is required by spec.
critical_code_review_comments: proxy.ts: API routes outside the small allowlist get redirected to /login HTML instead of returning JSON 401. This is a minor design smell for a JSON API.

register/route.ts: First-user-becomes-admin uses prisma.user.count()===1 AFTER creation. Race-safe enough for solo admin bootstrap, but two near-simultaneous first registrations could both see count==1 if better-auth's signUpEmail commits async. Acceptable for an MVP self-hosted app.

register/route.ts: After signUpEmail succeeds, the subsequent prisma.user.update + createMany + appointment-type create are NOT in a transaction. If any later step fails, the user row exists in inconsistent state. Wrap in prisma.$transaction.
ai/credentials/route.ts: POST upsert key is (userId, providerId, label). Multiple labels per provider is allowed; GET returns all credentials but UI must disambiguate.
integrations/google/connect/route.ts: HMAC signature truncated to 16 hex chars (64 bits). Acceptable for short-lived OAuth state but consider full-length hex for stronger anti-tamper.
providers-list/route.ts: Static array of 8 providers; OIDC enterprise SSO entry from .env (OIDC_*) is not exposed here even though env vars exist.
test_credentials: admin@cally.local / TestPass123 (existing seed); test creates a fresh test_<uuid>@cally.local each run.
seed_data_creation: Each test run creates one new user via /api/auth/register (email test_<random>@cally.local) and one OpenAI AI credential row (apiKeyEnc, defaultModel=gpt-5.2, test=false). No cleanup performed — Postgres data accumulates harmlessly.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pytest file at /app/backend/tests/test_cally_backend.py covers all 14 spec items. Use BASE_URL env var to override the preview URL if needed (defaults to the embedded preview URL). Tests assume Postgres is reachable at 127.0.0.1:5432 with cally/cally_local_dev. The `test_get_unauth_blocked` tests accept BOTH 401 and 307→/login so they will keep passing whether or not main agent fixes the middleware. If middleware is updated to skip /api/*, tighten those assertions to 401 only.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no failing functionality. The single behavioral discrepancy (middleware 307 vs route 401 for unauth API calls) is documented as a minor design issue with a recommended fix in /app/src/proxy.ts.
